# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains architectural claims, implementation details, benchmark definitions, reported benchmark results, and explicit limitations for a hierarchical multi-agent framework.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-07-048
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: Zhang et al., AgentOrchestra, Abstract/Section 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.12508v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
AgentOrchestra uses a two-tier hierarchical architecture with a top-level planning agent that decomposes tasks and coordinates specialized sub-agents.

## Source Excerpt

"AgentOrchestra features a central planning agent that decomposes complex objectives and delegates sub-tasks to a team of specialized agents." Also: "The framework adopts a two-tier architecture, where a top-level planning agent decomposes tasks and coordinates modular sub-agents responsible for domain-specific processing and multimodal reasoning."
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-07-048
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: method
citation: Zhang et al., AgentOrchestra, Section 3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.12508v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The planning agent maintains global progress awareness and updates plans dynamically using feedback from sub-agents and intermediate results.

## Source Excerpt

"A distinctive feature of the Planning Agent is its ability to maintain a global perspective throughout the execution process, aggregating feedback from sub-agents and monitoring progress toward the overall objective. This enables the agent to perform dynamic plan updates, adapting its strategy in real time in response to intermediate results, unexpected challenges, or shifting user requirements."
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-07-048
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: method
citation: Zhang et al., AgentOrchestra, Section 3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.12508v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The framework includes a unified LLM abstraction layer that supports both commercial and local open-source models and allows model switching during task execution.

## Source Excerpt

"we introduce a unified LLM abstraction layer that supports both leading commercial models (e.g., GPT-4.1, Claude-4-Sonnet) and local open-source models (e.g., Qwen2.5). This design enables agents to dynamically select and switch between different LLMs during task execution"
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-07-048
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: method
citation: Zhang et al., AgentOrchestra, Section 3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.12508v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Operations with potential side effects are executed inside a docker-based sandbox such as an isolated Linux container or virtual machine.

## Source Excerpt

"To ensure safety and system integrity, all operations with potential side effects are executed within a docker-based sandbox, such as an isolated Linux container or virtual machine."
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-07-048
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: method
citation: Zhang et al., AgentOrchestra, Section 3.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.12508v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The instantiated system includes three specialized sub-agents: Deep Researcher, Browser Use, and Deep Analyzer, and each sub-agent also has a Python interpreter tool.

## Source Excerpt

"First, a Deep Researcher Agent is responsible for extensive information retrieval... Next, a Browser Use Agent enables fine-grained interactions with web content... Finally, a Deep Analyzer Agent is designed to perform advanced reasoning and comprehensive analysis..." Also: "each sub-agent is also equipped with a python interpreter tool"
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-07-048
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric
citation: Zhang et al., AgentOrchestra, Section 4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.12508v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The SimpleQA benchmark used in evaluation contains 4,326 adversarially constructed fact-seeking questions.

## Source Excerpt

"SimpleQA ... consists of 4,326 adversarially constructed, fact-seeking questions spanning various domains"
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-07-048
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Zhang et al., AgentOrchestra, Section 4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.12508v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The GAIA benchmark used in evaluation contains 450 questions across three difficulty levels and includes web browsing, document analysis, and interactive reasoning scenarios.

## Source Excerpt

"GAIA ... comprises 450 questions spanning three difficulty levels and includes scenarios such as web browsing, document analysis, and interactive reasoning."
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-07-048
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: metric
citation: Zhang et al., AgentOrchestra, Section 4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.12508v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The HLE benchmark used in evaluation contains 2,500 multimodal questions spanning a wide range of subjects.

## Source Excerpt

"Humanity’s Last Exam (HLE) ... comprises 2,500 questions spanning a wide range of subjects"
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-07-048
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: method
citation: Zhang et al., AgentOrchestra, Section 4.4
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.12508v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The reported implementation uses claude-3.7-sonnet for the Planning Agent and Deep Researcher Agent, gpt-4.1 for the Browser Use Agent, and gemini-2.5-pro-preview-05-06 plus o3 for the Deep Analyzer Agent, with explicit step caps for each.

## Source Excerpt

"we utilize claude-3.7-sonnet with the thinking mode as the backbone for both the Planning Agent and the Deep Researcher Agent. For the Planning Agent, we cap the maximum number of steps at 20, while for the Deep Researcher Agent, the step limit is set to 3. The Browser Use Agent is implemented using gpt-4.1, with a maximum of 5 reasoning steps per episode. For the Auto Browser Use Tool, the maximum number of interaction steps per single invocation is set to 50. The Deep Analyzer Agent leverages both gemini-2.5-pro-preview-05-06 and o3 models, with the maximum steps limited to 3."
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-07-048
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: result
citation: Zhang et al., AgentOrchestra, Table 1 / Section 4.6
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.12508v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
On SimpleQA, the paper reports 95.3% accuracy for AgentOrchestra, compared with 93.9% for Perplexity Deep Research, 50.8% for gemini-2.5-pro-preview-05-06, and 49.4% for o3 without tools.

## Source Excerpt

Table 1 reports: "Perplexity Deep Research 93.9" and "Ours 95.3" under SimpleQA; the Models rows include "o3 (w/o tools) 49.4" and "gemini-2.5-pro-preview-05-06 50.8". Section 4.6 states: "our hierarchical agent framework achieves state-of-the-art performance on the SimpleQA benchmark, with an accuracy of 95.3%."
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-07-048
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: result
citation: Zhang et al., AgentOrchestra, Table 1 / Section 4.7
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.12508v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
On GAIA, the paper reports AgentOrchestra accuracies of 92.45% on Level 1, 83.72% on Level 2, 57.69% on Level 3, and 82.42% average.

## Source Excerpt

Table 1 lists for "Ours" on GAIA: "92.45 83.72 57.69 82.42". Section 4.7 states: "AgentOrchestra achieves state-of-the-art results on the GAIA validation dataset, with accuracies of 92.45% on Level 1, 83.72% on Level 2, and 57.69% on Level 3, for an overall average of 82.42%."
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-07-048
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: result
citation: Zhang et al., AgentOrchestra, Table 1 / Section 4.8 / Section 5
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.12508v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
On HLE, the paper reports 25.9% for AgentOrchestra, but the source also states that added architectural complexity and inter-agent communication can increase latency and computational overhead.

## Source Excerpt

Table 1 reports "Ours 25.9" for HLE; Section 4.8 states: "Our hierarchical agent achieves an average score of 25.9% on the HLE benchmark". Section 5 states: "the increased architectural complexity and inter-agent communication can result in additional system latency and computational overhead"

## Note

The source pairs its reported benchmark gains with an explicit limitation: the same hierarchical multi-agent design can add latency and overhead, especially relative to tasks that a single model could handle directly.
---

The source pairs its reported benchmark gains with an explicit limitation: the same hierarchical multi-agent design can add latency and overhead, especially relative to tasks that a single model could handle directly.

## Summary

This source contains architectural claims, implementation details, benchmark definitions, reported benchmark results, and explicit limitations for a hierarchical multi-agent framework.

AgentOrchestra uses a two-tier hierarchical architecture with a top-level planning agent that decomposes tasks and coordinates specialized sub-agents.

## Source Excerpt

"AgentOrchestra features a central planning agent that decomposes complex objectives and delegates sub-tasks to a team of specialized agents." Also: "The framework adopts a two-tier architecture, where a top-level planning agent decomposes tasks and coordinates modular sub-agents responsible for domain-specific processing and multimodal reasoning."
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---

contradiction_relevance: none
---
The planning agent maintains global progress awareness and updates plans dynamically using feedback from sub-agents and intermediate results.

## Source Excerpt

"A distinctive feature of the Planning Agent is its ability to maintain a global perspective throughout the execution process, aggregating feedback from sub-agents and monitoring progress toward the overall objective. This enables the agent to perform dynamic plan updates, adapting its strategy in real time in response to intermediate results, unexpected challenges, or shifting user requirements."
```

The framework includes a unified LLM abstraction layer that supports both commercial and local open-source models and allows model switching during task execution.

## Source Excerpt

"we introduce a unified LLM abstraction layer that supports both leading commercial models (e.g., GPT-4.1, Claude-4-Sonnet) and local open-source models (e.g., Qwen2.5). This design enables agents to dynamically select and switch between different LLMs during task execution"
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-07-048
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: method
citation: Zhang et al., AgentOrchestra, Section 4.4
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.12508v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The reported implementation uses claude-3.7-sonnet for the Planning Agent and Deep Researcher Agent, gpt-4.1 for the Browser Use Agent, and gemini-2.5-pro-preview-05-06 plus o3 for the Deep Analyzer Agent, with explicit step caps for each.

## Source Excerpt

"we utilize claude-3.7-sonnet with the thinking mode as the backbone for both the Planning Agent and the Deep Researcher Agent. For the Planning Agent, we cap the maximum number of steps at 20, while for the Deep Researcher Agent, the step limit is set to 3. The Browser Use Agent is implemented using gpt-4.1, with a maximum of 5 reasoning steps per episode. For the Auto Browser Use Tool, the maximum number of interaction steps per single invocation is set to 50. The Deep Analyzer Agent leverages both gemini-2.5-pro-preview-05-06 and o3 models, with the maximum steps limited to 3."
```
